rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical fluoroazotion of gem-difluoroalkenes with pyrazoles and Et3N·3HF to give trifluoromethyl-substituted N-alkylated pyrazoles
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the gem-difluorostyrene 1a to a radical cation, which is then attacked by fluoride (from Et3N·3HF) and pyrazole nucleophile

key_intermediates.name: benzylic α-fluoro carbocation/radical after F- addition
α-OOH, α-OMe, α-F peroxide adduct (compound 13) from O2/MeOH trapping

electron_flow: The anode oxidizes the gem-difluorostyrene to a radical cation; fluoride from Et3N·3HF adds to give a benzylic radical/cation that is further oxidized and trapped by the pyrazole N-H nucleophile, while protons are reduced at the Pt cathode to H2.
rate_determining_step: Anodic single-electron oxidation of the gem-difluorostyrene

probe_reagents.name: MeOH/O2 (radical trapping)
probe_reagents.observation: GC-MS detected peroxide adduct 13 (C15H14F2O3, exact mass 280.0911), consistent with a benzylic radical intermediate trapped by O2 and MeOH

probe_reagents.name: 4-vinylbiphenyl (non-fluorinated styrene control)
probe_reagents.observation: Easily oxidized under the conditions (lower oxidation potential by CV) but gave only messy mixtures with no fluorination product, indicating the gem-difluoro group is essential for selectivity

notes: CV of 1a, Et3N·3HF and product 3a recorded in nBu4NBF4/HFIP-PhCF3 shows 1a is oxidized within the operating window; control with normal styrene (4-vinylbiphenyl) demonstrates that the CF2 group is required to stabilize/direct the radical-cation intermediate. Optimization shows DCM/HFIP or PhCF3/HFIP mixed solvent and graphite(+)/Pt(-) electrodes are critical; no electricity gives 0% yield, confirming electrochemical initiation.
